topic: CLI-fallback feasibility for the mk_code_index MCP server (system-code-graph, 8 tools) — dual-stack scope: the MCP registration STAYS; the CLI is the resilience + universal surface for hooks, cron, CI, and transport-down recovery. PREMISE (settled, do not relitigate): the spec-memory record at .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/027-xce-research-based-refinement/004-shared-infrastructure/001-mcp-to-cli-tool-transition/001-spec-memory-cli/000-spec-memory-cli-research/research/research.md (sections 1-14) established the generic CLI-over-daemon pattern — thin IPC client, auto-spawn via the existing launcher, exit map 0/1/64/69/75 (75 retryable: -32001/SQLITE_BUSY/conn-fail; 69 protocol-mismatch fail-closed), subcommand codegen from the canonical tool registry, warm-only hook policy (~40-46ms p95 node start, 0.48ms IPC RTT measured on this host), dual-client bridge safety, owner-lease single-writer discipline. READ IT AS INPUT. Your mission: investigate the mk_code_index-SPECIFIC deltas only, with file:line or measured evidence from this repository; read-only bash timing measurements allowed, file mutation outside your artifact dir is not. Architecture anchors (verified): canonical registry CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS in .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts (~lines 186-195: code_graph_scan, code_graph_query, code_graph_status, code_graph_context, code_graph_classify_query_intent, code_graph_verify, code_graph_apply, detect_changes); hand-coded validateToolArgs (~213-260, JSON-schema NOT Zod); launcher .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs (966 lines: bootstrap lock ~714-757, owner lease ~284-404, shares launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs with mk-spec-memory); mcp_server/index.ts (IPC socket server ~149-154, idle monitor ~140-148, 20s lease heartbeat); state: code-graph.sqlite + leases + readiness marker (SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR); plugin transport cache TTL ~5s in .opencode/plugins/mk-code-graph.js; ZERO existing CLI today. The seed register — one focus per iteration, 10 forced iterations: KQ1 parity matrix, all 8 tools: STATELESS vs STATE-DAEMON classification, CLI subcommand mapping per tool, which need a live daemon vs direct-DB read-only access. KQ2 daemon-dependency audit: enumerate what dies per architecture (pure per-invocation CLI vs CLI-over-daemon vs CLI-with-auto-spawn) — owner-lease heartbeat, idle monitor, plugin transport cache, readiness marker, any watcher/queue. KQ3 affordance transfer: which spec-memory answers port verbatim (exit map, auto-spawn, --session-id, --format, --timeout-ms) and which do NOT — validateToolArgs is hand-coded JSON-schema, so quantify the codegen path difference vs spec-memory's Zod reuse. KQ4 prior-art deep-dive: mk-code-index-launcher.cjs as the spawn template; doctor:update orchestrator usage of code_graph_scan/verify; confirm zero existing CLI and what the nearest substitute is. KQ5 long-running ops: measure or bound code_graph_scan duration (cold full-scan vs incremental) — does it fit per-call CLI semantics or need async job semantics (start/status/cancel like memory_ingest); same question for code_graph_apply recovery actions. KQ6 integration-surface map MEASURED: exact files/counts of mk_code_index tool references across agents (context/deep-review/deep-research), session-prime hooks per runtime, plugin, doctor routes, deep-loop allowlists, opencode.json. KQ7 hook-latency fit: which hook contexts call code_graph_status/session-prime today, their timeout ceilings, and whether a warm CLI shell-out fits under the measured overhead baseline. KQ8 dual-stack coexistence + races: the code-graph lease implementation (MK_CODE_INDEX_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER), dual-simultaneous CLI spawn behavior, orphan reaping on a CLI-spawn path (idle monitor semantics when no MCP session exists). KQ9 risk register + named design deltas (D-series) an implementation phase must absorb, each with file/mechanism/acceptance. KQ10 verdict synthesis: go/no-go, architecture pick, bottom-up effort estimate, explicit inheritance list, and corrections to any spec-memory assumption that did not transfer. Every KQ must end terminally answered with evidence; write per-iteration findings and a final verdict-shaped research.md in your artifact dir.
